title: impatiens cribbii
intro: Impatiens cribbii, also known as the balsam apple or orange jewelweed, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat skin irritations and wounds. The plant is also believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Impatiens cribbii can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
